sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient, Brandon, a male, presents with neck pain, which started after a car crash in February of this year.

He reports experiencing sharp, incapacitating pain, rating it a 12 out of 10, with associated stiffness, headaches, dizziness, visual disturbances, numbness in the left arm and right leg, and spasms throughout his body.
He has a history of multiple car crashes, including one in which he underwent surgery, and has been experiencing pain since the most recent crash.
He has been to therapy and the pain clinic, where he was prescribed fentanyl, but has not received a prescription for several weeks.
Past medical history: The patient has a history of multiple car crashes, including one with surgery, and has been experiencing pain since the most recent crash.

Review of systems: The patient reports experiencing fatigue since the accident, and has associated symptoms of neck pain, including stiffness, headaches, dizziness, visual disturbances, numbness in the left arm and right leg, and spasms throughout his body.
Current medications: [NOT MENTIONED]
Objective:
Vital signs: [NOT MENTIONED]
Physical exam: The patient has pain on palpation of the neck, both on the bony process and on the muscle.
He is unable to move his neck from side to side or bend it forward and backward due to pain.
There is no bruising, swelling, or laceration noted on the neck.
Test Results: [NOT MENTIONED]
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has a neck sprain, which is not a fracture.
Problem list: Neck sprain; active; confirmed
Plan:
I prescribed robaxin 1500mg, to be taken every 6-8 hours, to help relax the muscles in the area and take off some of the tension and pain.
I will refer the patient to physical therapy and schedule an MRI to evaluate the extent of the injury.
If the patient's pain is not manageable with robaxin, I will consider referring him to pain management for local injections and further treatment.
Follow-up: The patient will be given a work excuse and will return for follow-up once the MRI results are available.
